title: Typing E. coli ATCC 11303 with fluorescently stained virus.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6gorl","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) with any additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow a 4 hour culture of ATCC 11303 in Fe-free LB by inoculating ATCC 11303 from an overnight culture grown in Fe-Free media into fresh media. 04:00:00, ATCC 11303 culture
Add 4 hour ATCC 11303 culture into a sterile 1.5 mL tube. undetermined, of 4 hour ATCC 11303 culture
Add YO-PRO stained T5. undetermined, YO-PRO add 10 ul DAPI, if needed.
Allow culture to sit in the dark at room temperature. 00:15:00
Add 500 μl of 10 mM MgSO 4 . undetermined, of 10 mM
In centrifuge, spin cells down at ~ 10,000 rpm, for 3 minutes. 00:03:00, at 10,000 rpm
Gently remove the supernatant with a pipette, then re-suspend the bacterial pellet in 1.5 ml of 10 mM MgSO 4 . undetermined, of 10 mM
Filter the sample onto 0.2 μm pore-size, black polycarbonate filter.
Rinse the filter gently, three times, with 1 ml of 10 mM MgSO 4 . undetermined, of 10 mM

Finally, with a clean slide and cover glass, pipet a drop of glycerol or immersion oil in the middle of the glass slide and place the cover glass on top of it. Let the glycerol spread out evenly under the cover glass, removing any bubbles. Remove the cover slip from the slide, place the filter on the slide and replace the cover slip.
creator: Ashley A Humphrey
